name: phase3-debug-camera
description: Guide for debugging camera stream issues

action.message:
Debug the Phase 3 camera stream. Check in this order:

1. CAMERA STATUS — check /vision_status endpoint:
   curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/vision_status
   Look for: running=true, frames>0, staleness<2.0, error=null

2. VEHICLE REACHABLE — verify DeepRacer is on the network:
   ping $DEEPRACER_IP
   Check DEEPRACER_IP in .env matches the car's actual IP

3. STREAM URL — the camera stream uses get_raw_video_stream() from aws-deepracer-control-v2
   The patched method in deepracer_tools.py uses: /route?topic=/camera_pkg/display_mjpeg&width=480&height=360
   Verify the patch is applied: drctl.Client.get_raw_video_stream = _get_raw_video_stream_patched

4. RECONNECT — if stream stopped, try:
   curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/camera/reconnect

5. REINIT — if init failed entirely:
   curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/reinit

6. FRAME STALENESS — if frames>0 but staleness is high:
   Stream is running but car may have rebooted. Try /camera/reconnect.

7. EXECUTION WITHOUT CAMERA — camera is advisory. If stream fails:
   _should_assess() still runs but get_latest_frame() returns None → 'continue'
   Execution proceeds normally without vision checks.

Report what you find and suggest fixes.
